title: Housing Help — Uma Kumaran
text_excerpt: Housing Help — Uma Kumaran 0 Skip to Content About me Newsletter Working hard for Stratford & Bow Speaking up in Parliament Policy Survey Contact Open Menu Close Menu About me Newsletter Working hard for Stratford & Bow Speaking up in Parliament Policy Survey Contact Open Menu Close Menu About me Newsletter Working hard for Stratford & Bow Speaking up in Parliament Policy Survey Contact Need Help With Housing? Note: Newham Council and Tower Hamlets Council have responsibility for the majority of housing issues including allocations, repairs and problems with landlords. If you are not satisfied with their response you may contact me, however please try to include the Council's response in your email. How I can help: ✅ I can make representations to housing associations or local councils where repairs have been delayed or refused, despite obligations. ✅ I can refer your case to the Housing Ombudsman if you have completed the final stage of your housing provider’s complaints process. ✅ I can raise concerns on your behalf where poor property management is affecting your health, safety, or wellbeing. What I can’t do: ❌ I cannot change an outcome where the council has offered accommodation. ❌ I cannot arrange housing for you if you are homeless. ❌ I cannot intervene with or change your position on a housing waiting list. FAQs I am unsatisfied with my position on a housing waiting list, can you help? I cannot intervene with or change your position on a housing waiting list. I would encourage anyone in this position to continue to bid on available properties. I am able to contact housing providers and the council on your behalf but I cannot change given outcomes. I’m having issues with repairs and maintenance, can you help? If you are living in a council property please contact your local councillor first. If you are in a housing association property please contact your housing provider first. If you are unsatisfied with the response from your landlord or housing provider please get in contact with my office where we can raise the issue on your behalf. Need to Discuss Your Situation with Me? The quickest way to contact me is by email at uma.kumaran.mp@parliament.uk Speak to my office by calling 020 7219 7777 Write to Uma at: House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA. Please include your: Full name Full Address and Postcode (Stratford and Bow) Information about the problem you’re facing. My team and I can only help if you provide all this information as, in line with parliamentary protocol, she can only help those living in Stratford and Bow. Find out if she is your MP here . Privacy policy Privacy policy Promoted by Aline Delawa on behalf of Uma Kumaran, both at 57-59 Great Suffolk Street, SE1 0BB Office standards Office standards
